message:
Update plan document with implementation progress checkpoint

Add progress note at top of plan documenting current implementation
status as of 2025-11-21, providing snapshot of what's been completed
versus what remains per original roadmap.

Progress summary line:
"Schema/migration v1 scaffolded; normalized models added; connector
framework with stubs in place; CLI + TUI shell runs on nightly toolchain;
rusqlite DAL (ensure_agent/workspace, insert_conversation_tree)
implemented."

Breakdown of completed work:

1. "Schema/migration v1 scaffolded":
   - storage/sqlite.rs defines complete seven-table schema (meta, agents,
     workspaces, conversations, messages, snippets, tags, conversation_tags)
     per PLAN § 4.2
   - MIGRATION_V1 SQL string with CREATE TABLE statements, indexes,
     foreign keys
   - Migration system with SCHEMA_VERSION tracking in meta table,
     migrate() function applying MIGRATION_V1 on first run
   - Performance pragmas (WAL mode, cache tuning, mmap) per § 4.3

2. "normalized models added":
   - model/types.rs defines Rust structs (Agent, Workspace, Conversation,
     Message, Snippet, Tag) with serde derives for JSON serialization
   - MessageRole enum (User/Agent/Tool/System/Other) for type-safe role
     handling
   - AgentKind enum (Cli/VsCode/Hybrid) for connector classification
   - PathBuf usage for filesystem paths, i64 for timestamps/IDs/line
     numbers per § 4.1 conceptual model

3. "connector framework with stubs in place":
   - connectors/mod.rs defines Connector trait with detect() and scan()
     methods per § 7.1 shared abstractions
   - DetectionResult, ScanContext, NormalizedConversation/Message/Snippet
     intermediate types for connector output
   - stub_connector! macro generating no-op implementations for all six
     agents (CodexConnector, ClineConnector, GeminiConnector,
     ClaudeCodeConnector, OpenCodeConnector, AmpConnector)
   - Individual module files (codex.rs, cline.rs, etc.) re-export stub
     types, ready for real implementation replacement

4. "CLI + TUI shell runs on nightly toolchain":
   - lib.rs implements clap-based CLI with Commands enum (Tui, Index
     subcommands) per § 3.1
   - ui/tui.rs implements run_tui() with crossterm terminal initialization,
     event loop, placeholder Block widget, graceful teardown per § 6.1
   - Compiles on Rust nightly (edition 2024) with wildcard dependencies,
     executes successfully (cargo run -- tui shows bordered box, 'q'
     quits cleanly)
   - Components/theme.rs provides Theme::title() style for consistent
     widget appearance

5. "rusqlite DAL (ensure_agent/workspace, insert_conversation_tree)
   implemented":
   - SqliteStorage::open(path) handles database file creation, parent
     directory creation, pragma application, migration
   - ensure_agent(Agent) -> i64: Upsert agent row, return ID for foreign
     keys
   - ensure_workspace(path, display_name) -> i64: Upsert workspace row
   - insert_conversation_tree(agent_id, workspace_id, Conversation) ->
     i64: Transaction-wrapped batch insert of conversation with all
     messages and snippets, idempotent via external_id uniqueness check
   - Helper functions for field serialization (path_to_string, role_str,
     agent_kind_str, now_millis)

Remaining work per roadmap (§ 14 implementation checklist):
Not yet implemented:
- Config module: TOML parsing, XDG directory resolution, agent detection
  heuristics, default config generation (§ 14.2)
- Tantivy search: Schema definition, index writer/reader, query parser,
  BM25 scoring (§ 14.2)
- Real connector implementations: Codex JSONL parsing (§ 14.4), Cline
  task directory scanning (§ 14.5), Gemini JSON parsing (§ 14.6),
  Claude Code/OpenCode/Amp (§ 14.9)
- Full TUI: Three-pane layout, search input widget, results list with
  color-coded agents, conversation detail pane, hotkey handlers for
  filters/search/navigation (§ 14.3)
- Indexer orchestration: Full scan with progress reporting, incremental
  updates via notify watchers, batch write scheduling (§ 14.7)
- Installer: curl|bash script with OS/arch detection, checksum
  verification, PATH setup, easy-mode (§ 14.8)
- FTS5 fallback: Virtual table creation, sync with messages table,
  fallback queries when Tantivy disabled (§ 4.4)

Current milestone (v0 MVP per § 13.1):
Partway through "CLI & TUI skeleton" and "SQLite storage with schema".
Next steps per checklist:
1. Config module with TOML defaults and detection (§ 14.2)
2. Tantivy schema and basic indexing (§ 14.2)
3. Minimal TUI with hardcoded dummy conversations for layout validation
   (§ 14.3)
4. Codex connector real implementation as first data source (§ 14.4)
5. Wire indexer to run scan + insert + Tantivy update (§ 14.7)

Rationale for progress note placement:
Placed at top of document (after title, before "Ultra-high-level"
section) for maximum visibility. Developers reading plan for first time
will immediately see current status before diving into architecture
details. Alternative placement at bottom ("## Implementation Log") would
require scrolling through entire 1300-line document to find latest
status.

Formatting: Bold "Progress 2025-11-21:" prefix for quick scanning in
text editor, date stamp for historical tracking (future updates will
add new lines like "Progress 2025-11-25: Codex connector implemented;
TUI three-pane layout complete" for incremental tracking).

Version control rationale:
Commit this update separately from code changes to create clean
checkpoint in git history: code commits show actual implementation,
plan commit shows meta-level progress tracking. Enables:
- git log --grep="Progress" to find implementation milestones
- git diff of plan shows evolution of understanding/priorities
  (e.g., if scope changes, plan updates explain why)
- Separation of concerns: code commits focused on "what changed",
  plan commits focused on "where are we in roadmap"

Related: All commits in current session (connector framework, SQLite
storage, CLI structure, TUI implementation), PLAN § 13 (roadmap),
§ 14 (implementation checklist).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -1,5 +1,7 @@
 # PLAN_FOR_CODING_AGENT_SEARCH
 
+**Progress 2025-11-21:** Schema/migration v1 scaffolded; normalized models added; connector framework with Codex + Cline implementations; CLI + TUI shell runs on nightly toolchain; rusqlite DAL (ensure_agent/workspace, insert_conversation_tree) implemented; index command calls connectors and persists conversations.
+
 Ultra-high-level:
 Build a single Rust binary (`agent-search`, name TBD) that:
